message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE BUILDER (Opus hands) for "compose". Fable already found the problems and
told you EXACTLY what to change. DO NOT re-investigate, DO NOT hunt for an entrypoint, DO NOT wander.
Apply ONLY these fixes to scripts/run_s5_i3.py, in order:
[
  {
    "severity": "P0",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:342-359 (add setdefault) / src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:3390,3664-3676",
    "problem": "strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL in production: _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset (VERIFIED read at provenance_generator.py:3390; serial gate `if _verify_workers < 2` at :3664), and the knob is set in no compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. The bounded ThreadPoolExecutor path (:3678-3718, contextvars-copied, cost-reconciled, already gated at 16 in scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py) is dead code in production. strict_verify runs twice per section (:5667, :5804).",
    "fix": "Add `os.environ.setdefault(\"PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY\", \"8\")` to the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 block and `export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh. Zero code change; the parallel path preserves original-order reassembly so kept/dropped verdicts are byte-identical to serial.",
    "acceptance": "Log/probe shows _parallel_verify_workers() == 8 and strict_verify enters the ThreadPoolExecutor branch (knob actually read); strict_verify wall time for a multi-sentence section drops several-fold; kept/dropped verdict set identical to a serial control on the same fixture."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:325 (residual plan) and :491,:588-597 (sec_sema + task order)",
    "problem": "94% of baskets (304-326 of 346) land in ONE residual section coroutine that is scheduled LAST behind Semaphore(2) — it starts 50-90 min in and is a single indivisible serial unit, so the run makespan is one serial section and PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS has nothing to parallelize. (Dedups 2 findings; do AFTER the env+await commits.)",
    "fix": "Two independent changes in run_s5_i3.py: (1) shard the residual section's baskets into ceil(N/16)-basket sub-sections composed as separate tasks under sec_sema (basket rendering is independent; reassemble by index, dedup on the concatenated list) and raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS to ~4-6 to match; (2) create section tasks sorted by len(section.ev_ids) descending so the biggest work starts at t=0. Also checkpoint at basket/shard granularity so a kill does not discard 95 min of work.",
    "acceptance": "Log shows the residual section's shards STARTing at t~0 and >1 concurrent '[section N] START' with N>=4; total baskets processed across shards == 346 with no dupes/drops in the concatenated output; makespan no longer equals the residual section's solo time."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:506-523 and launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh (input pinning)",
    "problem": "SHA-gated section RESUME never fires (0/5 attempts): a root daemon outside this worktree (/workspace/gear_sync.sh) overwrites the pinned cp3 path ~every 96 min while a 3h+ compose attempt runs, so every relaunch sees a SHA mismatch, declares all checkpoints stale, and recomposes from scratch — a livelock, not a slowdown. The daemon itself is outside the fence; fix must be entirely on our side.",
    "fix": "(a) In the launcher, COPY cp2/cp3/cp4 into the run's own output dir at attempt-1 start and pass the copies (immutable inputs for the attempt's lifetime; 'newest wins' applies between runs, not during one). (b) In run_s5_i3.py, namespace checkpoints per input-SHA (ckpt/<sha12>/section_N_draft.json) so a changed input opens a new namespace instead of discarding a valid one.",
    "acceptance": "Kill a run mid-wave and relaunch while gear_sync keeps mutating the shared cp3: relaunch logs resume-hit > 0 (loads at least the completed section_*_draft.json) and the inputs_sha check passes against the snapshot copies."
  }
]

For each: make the concrete change at the file:line; then ACTUALLY EXERCISE the touched path (run it,
py_compile is NOT enough — the acceptance observable tells you what to run); then git commit in
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix with an honest message (say PARTIAL if partial). Append one dated line to
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Report what landed vs what Fable asked. A skipped fix reported is fine; hidden is not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

300	        super().__init__(level=logging.INFO)
301	        self.counts = {k: 0 for k in self._PATTERNS}
302	        self.prepass_lines = []
303	
304	    def emit(self, record):
305	        try:
306	            msg = record.getMessage()
307	        except Exception:
308	            return
309	        for key, needle in self._PATTERNS.items():
310	            if needle in msg:
311	                self.counts[key] += 1
312	        if "pre-pass complete" in msg:
313	            self.prepass_lines.append(msg[:300])
314	
315	
316	async def main():
317	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
318	    ap.add_argument("--cp2", required=True)
319	    ap.add_argument("--cp3", required=True)
320	    ap.add_argument("--cp4", required=True)
321	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
322	    ap.add_argument("--only-section", type=int, default=-1)
323	    ap.add_argument("--sections", type=str, default="", help="comma list of section indices to compose (default all)")
324	    ap.add_argument("--ckpt-dir", type=str, default="", help="dir for per-section draft checkpoints (timeout-resilient)")
325	    ap.add_argument("--cap-primary", type=int, default=0, help="Fix 5 (2026-07-10): SMOKE-ONLY disclosed knob. Keep only first N primary views per section. DEFAULT 0 = ALL primary views = a full-coverage acceptance run (the gate round MUST run at 0 so coverage and defects 1/2/5 are judgeable). Any N>0 is an explicitly-disclosed bounded subset, never a full acceptance run.")
326	    args = ap.parse_args()
327	
328	    # Fix 5 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): configure INFO logging so the writer/activation markers
329	    # ([activation] synth_primary, [abstractive_writer] pre-pass complete, K-span fallbacks, judge
330	    # errors) reach the tee'd compose.log — the driver previously never called basicConfig, so only
331	    # WARNING+ was visible and the writer-yield collapse was undiagnosable. Also install the marker tally
332	    # so the counts land in the cp5 payload.
333	    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s")
334	    logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.INFO)
335	    marker_tally = _MarkerTally()
336	    logging.getLogger().addHandler(marker_tally)
337	
338	    # Fix 3 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): writer THROUGHPUT/RESILIENCE flags ON by default for this
339	    # driver so a transient transport disconnect never permanently kills a basket wave (the prior run
340	    # lost 3/5 sections' whole writer wave in 0-2s). setdefault => an explicit launcher/env value still
341	    # wins. General resilience knobs, not question-tuned; recorded in flag_slate for disclosure.
342	    for _k, _v in (
343	        ("PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE", "1"),
344	        ("PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED", "1"),
345	        ("PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS", "1"),
346	    ):
347	        os.environ.setdefault(_k, _v)
348	
349	    # Fix 6 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): entailment-judge RELIABILITY for compose runs. The prior run
350	    # showed "empty or non-str judge content" (a z-ai empty-200 blank window, NOT starvation — the judge
351	    # default max_tokens is already the provider chain MIN 131072 and effort "high", both the model max)
352	    # and "total_deadline_exceeded_150s". Route around blank-200 windows (PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE) and
353	    # grant a bounded larger total wall. Do NOT override PG_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS / _REASONING_EFFORT
354	    # (override clamps down / xhigh starves GLM per the mirror bake-off). setdefault => launcher/env wins.
355	    for _k, _v in (
356	        ("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", "1"),
357	        ("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S", "300"),
358	    ):
359	        os.environ.setdefault(_k, _v)
360	
361	    sel = set()
362	    if args.sections.strip():
363	        sel = {int(x) for x in args.sections.split(",") if x.strip() != ""}
364	    ckpt_dir = Path(args.ckpt_dir) if args.ckpt_dir.strip() else None
365	    if ckpt_dir:
366	        ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
367	
368	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import (
369	        SectionPlan,
370	        _run_section,
371	        # Fix 3 + Fix 8 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): the production gap-stub sentences, so the
372	        # assembler can render an honest gap for an empty body and vary a repeated generic stub.
373	        _GAP_STUB_SENTENCE,

392	    question = cp2.get("question") or ""
393	    evidence_rows = cp2["evidence_for_gen"]
394	    evidence_pool = {str(r["evidence_id"]): r for r in evidence_rows if r.get("evidence_id")}

401	    baskets = build_baskets(cp3_baskets, evidence_pool)
402	    print(f"[baskets] reconstructed ClaimBaskets={len(baskets)} "
403	          f"(members total={sum(len(b.supporting_members) for b in baskets)})", flush=True)
404	
405	    # Populate credibility + origin coverage for EVERY pooled evidence_id (the disclosure coverage
406	    # assertion in credibility_pass.apply_disclosure_to_svs is fail-LOUD: every cited evidence_id MUST
407	    # have both credibility_by_evidence AND origin_by_evidence coverage, else abort_credibility_coverage_gap).
408	    # In production _run_chain co-builds these per row; the offline harness must supply them. Neutral
409	    # weight 1.0 (harness) — the binding faithfulness gate is strict_verify per composed sentence, unchanged.
410	    cred_by_ev = {}
411	    origin_by_ev = {}
412	    cluster_id_by_ev = {}
413	    for eid in evidence_pool:
414	        eid = str(eid)
415	        cred_by_ev[eid] = EvidenceCredibility(
416	            evidence_id=eid, credibility_weight=1.0, reliability_score=1.0, relevance_score=1.0,
417	            origin_cluster_id=eid, is_canonical_origin=True, certainty_downgrade=False, soft_warning=None,
418	        )
419	        origin_by_ev[eid] = eid
420	    for b in baskets:

422	            cluster_id_by_ev.setdefault(str(mem.evidence_id), []).append(b.claim_cluster_id)
423	    cred = CredibilityAnalysis(

425	        weight_mass=[], baskets=baskets, cluster_id_by_evidence=cluster_id_by_ev,
426	    )
427	    print(f"[cred] coverage populated: credibility_by_evidence={len(cred_by_ev)} "
428	          f"origin_by_evidence={len(origin_by_ev)} cluster_id_by_evidence={len(cluster_id_by_ev)}", flush=True)
429	
430	    plans = [SectionPlan(title=str(p.get("title", "")), focus=str(p.get("focus", "")),
431	                         ev_ids=[str(e) for e in (p.get("ev_ids") or [])]) for p in plans_raw]
432	
433	    print(f"[map] section_basket_map_enabled={section_basket_map_enabled()} weights={resolve_weights()}", flush=True)
434	    sbm_map = build_section_basket_map(baskets, plans, evidence_pool=evidence_pool)
435	    per_sec_views = {int(k): len(v) for k, v in sbm_map.views_by_section.items()}
436	    print(f"[map] stranded={sbm_map.stranded_count} residual_index={sbm_map.residual_section_index} "
437	          f"nocid_synth={sbm_map.stats.get('nocid_synthetic_count', 0)} per_section_views={per_sec_views}", flush=True)
438	    # primary counts per section (the baskets each section will actually FULL-compose)
439	    prim_per_sec = {}

441	        prim_per_sec[int(idx)] = sum(1 for v in views if getattr(v, "role", "") == "primary")
442	    print(f"[map] primary_per_section={prim_per_sec}", flush=True)

444	    if args.cap_primary and args.cap_primary > 0:
445	        # BOUNDED read (disclosed): truncate each section's PRIMARY views to first N; keep all
446	        # corroborating. Lets a fast multi-section run fire the holistic cross-section pass on real
447	        # prose. This is a SUBSET, not a full acceptance run.
448	        for idx, views in list(sbm_map.views_by_section.items()):
449	            kept, nprim = [], 0
450	            for v in views:
451	                if getattr(v, "role", "") == "primary":
452	                    if nprim >= args.cap_primary:
453	                        continue
454	                    nprim += 1
455	                kept.append(v)
456	            sbm_map.views_by_section[idx] = kept

458	                        for k, vs in sbm_map.views_by_section.items()}
459	        print(f"[map] CAP-PRIMARY={args.cap_primary} -> primary_per_section={prim_per_sec}", flush=True)

461	    # Faithful production wiring (generate_multi_section_report): append the keep-all RESIDUAL
462	    # SectionPlan and attach the map + a stable section index onto every SectionPlan object, so this
463	    # branch's _section_baskets_for_compose consumes them via getattr (no _run_section sig change).
464	    _resid_idx = sbm_map.residual_section_index
465	    if _resid_idx is not None and _resid_idx == len(plans):
466	        _resid_ev = []
467	        _seen_re = set()

469	            for _re in getattr(_rv, 'section_member_ev_ids', None) or []:

472	                    _seen_re.add(_re_s)
473	                    _resid_ev.append(_re_s)
474	        plans.append(SectionPlan(
475	            title=sbm_map.residual_title or 'Additional Corroborated Findings',
476	            focus='Corroborated findings that did not bind to a primary outline section.',
477	            ev_ids=_resid_ev,
478	        ))
479	        print(f'[map] residual SectionPlan appended idx={_resid_idx} ev_ids={len(_resid_ev)}', flush=True)
480	    for _p_idx, _p_obj in enumerate(plans):
481	        try:
482	            _p_obj._section_index = _p_idx
483	            _p_obj._section_basket_map = sbm_map
484	        except Exception:
485	            pass
486	
487	    model = os.environ.get("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
488	    section_max_tokens = int(os.environ.get("PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", "64000"))
489	    section_temperature = float(os.environ.get("PG_SECTION_TEMPERATURE", "0.3"))
490	    min_kept_fraction = float(os.environ.get("PG_MIN_KEPT_FRACTION", "0.4"))
491	    sec_sema = asyncio.Semaphore(int(os.environ.get("PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "2")))
492	
493	    # Fix 9 (2026-07-11 compose gear-loop iter 4): SECTION-LEVEL crash RESUME. The iter-3 run (PID 768574)
494	    # was externally SIGKILLed at 08:19:57 ~1 min into a section finalization after 1h15m of compose; the
495	    # per-section checkpoints (written at section completion below) were NEVER read back, so the whole wave
496	    # was lost. This block LOADS any completed per-section checkpoint and SKIPS recomposing that section,
497	    # so a relaunch resumes at the closest checkpoint (operator ground rule 2026-07-01) and the gear loop
498	    # makes guaranteed forward progress across kills. Resume is GATED on the three input SHAs (cp2/cp3/cp4):
499	    # if the pinned corpus/outline changed since the checkpoints were written, the stale drafts are IGNORED
500	    # and every section recomposes (honors the gear rule = newest inputs win). Only a genuinely-composed
501	    # section is resumed (real verified prose, not a gap stub / degraded / errored section) — a transient-
502	    # failure section is retried, never banked (fail-open: any doubt => recompose).
503	    resumed: dict = {}
504	    if ckpt_dir:
505	        import types as _types_resume
506	        _in_sha = {
507	            "cp2": hashlib.sha256(Path(args.cp2).read_bytes()).hexdigest(),
508	            "cp3": hashlib.sha256(Path(args.cp3).read_bytes()).hexdigest(),
509	            "cp4": hashlib.sha256(Path(args.cp4).read_bytes()).hexdigest(),
510	        }
511	        _man = ckpt_dir / "inputs_sha.json"
512	        _stale = False
513	        if _man.exists():
514	            try:
515	                _prev = json.loads(_man.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
516	            except Exception:
517	                _prev = {}
518	            if (_prev.get("cp2") != _in_sha["cp2"] or _prev.get("cp3") != _in_sha["cp3"]
519	                    or _prev.get("cp4") != _in_sha["cp4"]):
520	                _stale = True
521	                print("[resume] input SHAs changed since checkpoint -> IGNORING stale section drafts, "
522	                      "recomposing all sections (gear rule: newest inputs win)", flush=True)
523	        _man.write_text(json.dumps(_in_sha, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
524	        if not _stale:
525	            for _ck in sorted(ckpt_dir.glob("section_*_draft.json")):
526	                try:
527	                    _d = json.loads(_ck.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
528	                except Exception as _e:
529	                    print(f"[resume] skip unreadable checkpoint {_ck.name}: {_e!r}", flush=True)
530	                    continue
531	                _idx = _d.get("section_index")
532	                _vt = (_d.get("verified_text") or "").strip()
533	                if (_idx is None or not _vt or _d.get("is_gap_stub")
534	                        or _d.get("dropped_due_to_failure") or _d.get("error")
535	                        or int(_d.get("sentences_verified") or 0) <= 0):
536	                    continue
537	                resumed[int(_idx)] = _types_resume.SimpleNamespace(
538	                    title=str(_d.get("title") or f"Section {_idx}"),
539	                    focus=str(_d.get("focus") or ""),
540	                    ev_ids_assigned=list(_d.get("ev_ids_assigned") or []),
541	                    verified_text=_d.get("verified_text") or "",
542	                    sentences_verified=int(_d.get("sentences_verified") or 0),
543	                    sentences_dropped=int(_d.get("sentences_dropped") or 0),
544	                    regen_attempted=bool(_d.get("regen_attempted")),
545	                    dropped_due_to_failure=bool(_d.get("dropped_due_to_failure")),
546	                    is_gap_stub=bool(_d.get("is_gap_stub")),
547	                    error=_d.get("error"),
548	                )
549	            if resumed:
550	                print(f"[resume] loaded {len(resumed)} completed section(s) from checkpoints: "
551	                      f"{sorted(resumed)} -- skipping recompose", flush=True)
552	
553	    async def _compose_one(idx, section):
554	        async with sec_sema:
555	            t0 = time.time()
556	            print(f"[section {idx}] START title={section.title!r} ev_ids={len(section.ev_ids)}", flush=True)
557	            res = await _run_section(
558	                section, evidence_pool,
559	                model=model,
560	                temperature=section_temperature,
561	                max_tokens_per_section=section_max_tokens,
562	                min_kept_fraction=min_kept_fraction,
563	                credibility_analysis=cred,
564	                research_question=question,
565	            )
566	            dt = time.time() - t0
567	            vt = res.verified_text or ""
568	            print(f"[section {idx}] DONE {dt:.0f}s verified_chars={len(vt)} "
569	                  f"sentences_verified={res.sentences_verified} dropped={res.sentences_dropped} "
570	                  f"regen={res.regen_attempted} dropped_fail={res.dropped_due_to_failure} "
571	                  f"gap_stub={getattr(res,'is_gap_stub',False)} error={res.error!r}", flush=True)
572	            print(f"[section {idx}] OPENING: {vt[:500]}", flush=True)
573	            if ckpt_dir:
574	                # timeout/crash-resilient per-section draft checkpoint (Design 4 §7c)
575	                (ckpt_dir / f"section_{idx}_draft.json").write_text(json.dumps({
576	                    "section_index": idx, "title": res.title, "focus": res.focus,
577	                    "ev_ids_assigned": res.ev_ids_assigned, "verified_text": vt,
578	                    "sentences_verified": res.sentences_verified,
579	                    "sentences_dropped": res.sentences_dropped,
580	                    "regen_attempted": res.regen_attempted,
581	                    "dropped_due_to_failure": res.dropped_due_to_failure,
582	                    "is_gap_stub": getattr(res, "is_gap_stub", False), "error": res.error,
583	                }, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
584	            return res
585	
586	    tasks = []
587	    order = []
588	    for idx, section in enumerate(plans):
589	        if args.only_section >= 0 and idx != args.only_section:
590	            continue
591	        if sel and idx not in sel:
592	            continue
593	        if idx in resumed:
594	            continue
595	        order.append(idx)
596	        tasks.append(asyncio.ensure_future(_compose_one(idx, section)))
597	    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
598	
599	    import types

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Compose gear-loop iter 5 — builds on iter 4 (section-level crash RESUME + self-relaunch). Two changes,
3	# both faithfulness-NEUTRAL (numeric forward-match + context-level NLI entailment UNCHANGED; the lexical
4	# >=2-word overlap gate stays DELETED at this branch head; nothing question-tuned):
5	#   (A) GEAR RULE (operator 2026-07-10): iter-4 HARDCODED the pinned cp3/cp4 paths. iter-5 RESOLVES the
6	#       NEWEST outline cp4 AND newest corpus cp3 at launch (ls -t) so the compose gear auto-picks up new
7	#       S3/S4 rounds as they land — the gears turn into each other. The pinned s4_gear/s3_gear ARE the
8	#       newest today, so pin + newest coincide; the dynamic resolve keeps that true on the NEXT S3/S4 round
9	#       without a launcher edit. Both resolved paths are PRINTED for the round report.
10	#   (B) 429 SELF-CONTENTION (observed in the iter-4 run 08:33-09:02: repeated OpenRouter 429 backoff +
11	#       a 3-min stall while a second heavy OpenRouter job ran in a sibling worktree). Drop the compose's
12	#       OpenRouter concurrency (PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 30->16, writer 24->12) so the gear loop coexists with
13	#       sibling jobs on the shared OpenRouter rate limit with FEWER backoffs -> sections FINALIZE and reach
14	#       the iter-4 per-section checkpoint sooner (a kill then loses less work). max_tokens slate unchanged
15	#       (a CAP, not a target -> reasoning is never starved; §9.1.8).
16	set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a
17	cd /workspace/compose_wt || exit 9
18	export PYTHONPATH=/workspace/compose_wt PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
19	# -- activation flags (ghost-free slate, iter-4 identical) --
20	export PG_COMPOSE_NO_RAW_SPAN_FALLBACK=1
21	export PG_SECTION_BASKET_MAP=1
22	export PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY=1
23	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER=1
24	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE=1
25	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED=1
26	export PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce
27	export PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE=1
28	export PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD=1
29	export PG_RENDER_SEAM_SANITIZE=1
30	# -- B4 resilience levers + throughput (iter-4 proven) --
31	export PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED=1
32	export PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS=1
33	# iter-5 (B): lower concurrency to cut 429 self-contention with sibling OpenRouter jobs.
34	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12
35	export PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16
36	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S=300
37	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S=1400
38	export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300
39	# -- §9.1.8 always-MAX token slate (z-ai/glm-5.2 real caps) --
40	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS=131072
41	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=65536
42	export PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000
43	export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2
44	# -- iter-5 (A): PINNED best inputs are the DEFAULT; the GEAR RULE resolves the NEWEST cp4+cp3 and uses
45	#    them when they are newer than the pin. Today pin == newest, so both resolve to s4_gear/s3_gear. --
46	CP2=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s2_hamster_i1/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json
47	PIN_CP3=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json
48	PIN_CP4=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_gear/cp4_outline_snapshot.json
49	NEW_CP4=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_*/cp4_outline_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
50	NEW_CP3=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_*/cp3_basket_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
51	CP4=${NEW_CP4:-$PIN_CP4}
52	CP3=${NEW_CP3:-$PIN_CP3}
53	OUT=/workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter5
54	CKPT=$OUT/ckpt
55	LOG=$OUT/compose.log
56	mkdir -p "$CKPT"
57	# -- FAIL-LOUD ghost-free pre-flight (operator P0 wiring lock) --
58	GHOST=$(grep -c writer_numeric_dropped src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py || true)
59	if [ "$GHOST" != "0" ]; then
60	  echo "[ABORT] ghost present: writer_numeric_dropped count=$GHOST" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 3
61	fi
62	if ! python -u -c "from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _synth_primary_enabled; import sys; sys.exit(0 if _synth_primary_enabled() else 7)"; then
63	  echo "[ABORT] _synth_primary_enabled() is False under this env slate" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 4
64	fi
65	echo "[preflight] ghost_absent(writer_numeric_dropped=0) AND synth_primary_enabled=True -> OK" | tee -a "$LOG"
66	{
67	  echo "=== iter-5 launch $(date -u) ==="
68	  echo "CP2=$CP2"; echo "CP3(resolved)=$CP3"; echo "CP4(resolved)=$CP4"
69	  echo "pin_cp3=$PIN_CP3 newest_cp3=$NEW_CP3"; echo "pin_cp4=$PIN_CP4 newest_cp4=$NEW_CP4"
70	  echo "commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)"
71	  echo "PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=$PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=$PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"
72	} | tee -a "$LOG"
73	for attempt in $(seq 1 8); do
74	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 present -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
75	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt START $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
76	  python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py     --cp2 "$CP2" --cp3 "$CP3" --cp4 "$CP4"     --out "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json"     --ckpt-dir "$CKPT"     --cap-primary 0 < /dev/null >> "$LOG" 2>&1
77	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt EXIT rc=$? $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
78	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 written -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
79	  sleep 5
80	done
81	echo "=== iter-5 loop finished $(date -u) cp5_exists=$([ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && echo yes || echo no) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
82

3380	#   - N >= 2                     => parallel with EXACTLY N concurrent judge calls.
3381	# N is a CONCURRENCY bound, never a per-claim TARGET — every sentence is still
3382	# verified; only how many judge calls are in flight at once changes.
3383	def _parallel_verify_workers() -> int:
3384	    """fix#19 (#1262). Resolve PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY to a bounded worker count.
3385	
3386	    Returns 1 (=> serial path, OFF) when unset/empty/0/1/negative/malformed, so the
3387	    default and any non-positive override is byte-identical to the legacy serial
3388	    loop. Returns N>=2 only for an explicit positive override, capping the number
3389	    of concurrent entailment-judge calls. Read at call time so tests can toggle."""
3390	    raw = os.getenv("PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY", "").strip()
3391	    if not raw:
3392	        return 1
3393	    try:
3394	        n = int(raw)
3395	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
3396	        return 1
3397	    return n if n >= 2 else 1
3398	
3399	
3400	def _is_content_empty_sentence(sentence: str) -> bool:
3401	    """I-pipe-016 (#1241). True iff `sentence` carries NO real claim content —
3402	    no content words AND no numeric values — after stripping citation artifacts
3403	    (provenance/calc tokens, atom_NNN, bare [ev_XXX]) and dose/placebo/threshold
3404	    structural residue.
3405	
3406	    Uses the SAME stripping the BUG-03 numeric/empty floor uses inside
3407	    verify_sentence_provenance, so the two definitions agree: a fragment this
3408	    returns True for is exactly the kind verify_sentence_provenance would drop
3409	    with `empty_or_contentless_sentence`. A sentence with ANY content word OR

3655	            _excluded_empty += 1
3656	            continue
3657	        _findings_to_verify.append(s)
3658	
3659	    # fix#19 (#1262): verify each surviving sentence — the SAME verify-or-rescue
3660	    # decision as before, just dispatched either serially (default) or across a
3661	    # BOUNDED thread pool. Verdicts are independent; results are reassembled in the
3662	    # ORIGINAL order so kept/dropped are byte-identical to the serial path.
3663	    _reanchor_enabled = _provenance_reanchor_enabled()
3664	    _verify_workers = _parallel_verify_workers()
3665	    if _verify_workers < 2 or len(_findings_to_verify) < 2:
3666	        # Serial path (default / single-item): byte-identical to the legacy loop.
3667	        _findings_results = [
3668	            _verify_one_findings_sentence(
3669	                s, evidence_pool,
3670	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3671	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3672	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3673	            )
3674	            for s in _findings_to_verify
3675	        ]
3676	    else:
3677	        # Parallel path: a BOUNDED ThreadPoolExecutor caps concurrency at exactly
3678	        # _verify_workers. A fresh worker thread starts with an EMPTY contextvars
3679	        # context, which would silently drop the per-run judge telemetry (FX-09
3680	        # ContextVar holding the run's {calls, judge_error} dict), the ambient role,
3681	        # and the gate's provider pin. So we capture the PARENT context ONCE here in
3682	        # the calling thread and run each task inside a copy of it — the ContextVar
3683	        # still references the SAME run-telemetry dict object, so the in-place
3684	        # ``_run_tel[...] += 1`` ticks land in the right counter. ``map`` preserves
3685	        # input order => the reassembled list is index-aligned with
3686	        # _findings_to_verify (deterministic kept/dropped, never race-ordered). A
3687	        # worker exception PROPAGATES out of map() (fail-loud — a hard gate /
3688	        # programming defect is never swallowed), exactly as the serial loop raises.
3689	        import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero OFF-path cost)
3690	        import contextvars as _ctxvars  # noqa: PLC0415
3691	
3692	        # I-arch-011 (Codex FIX-C P1): each worker runs in a COPIED context, so the entailment
3693	        # judge's ``_orc._add_run_cost`` / ``check_run_budget`` mutate the COPY's ``_RUN_COST_CTX``
3694	        # (lost to the parent) — the parallel verify spend would bypass PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and the
3695	        # run-budget gate. The cost LEDGER stays accurate (``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps a
3696	        # process-global, lock-protected per-session accumulator, NOT a contextvar). Mirror the
3697	        # credibility_pass offload reconciliation (openrouter_client.ledger_cumulative docstring):
3698	        # snapshot the per-session ledger cumulative before/after the pool and re-add the delta to the
3699	        # PARENT ``_RUN_COST_CTX``, then re-check the budget so the gate is inclusive of the parallel
3700	        # judge spend. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (cost accounting only; verdicts unchanged).
3701	        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc_cost  # noqa: PLC0415
3702	        _run_id = _orc_cost._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get()
3703	        _cost_before = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id)
3704	
3705	        _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()
3706	
3707	        def _verify_in_context(_s: str) -> SentenceVerification:
3708	            return _parent_ctx.copy().run(
3709	                _verify_one_findings_sentence,
3710	                _s, evidence_pool,
3711	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3712	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3713	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3714	            )
3715	
3716	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_verify_workers) as _pool:
3717	            _findings_results = list(_pool.map(_verify_in_context, _findings_to_verify))
3718	
3719	        # Reconcile the parallel verify spend into the parent budget gate (see note above).
3720	        _cost_delta = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id) - _cost_before
3721	        if _cost_delta > 0:
3722	            _orc_cost._add_run_cost(_cost_delta)
3723	            _orc_cost.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the pool breached the cap
3724

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-011 FINAL behavioral gate (§-1.4): the enrichment-section strict_verify under the REAL judge
3	+ PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=16 (FIX-C) on the banked drb_78 corpus. Proves the section COMPLETES in minutes
4	(run #6 took ~173 min serial), KEEPS >=200 cited sources on the real enforce path, and quantifies the
5	junk-screen leakage (metadata / URL / off-topic units the judge entails but aren't useful citations).
6	
7	This is the real-output proof the deploy rides on. ~1839 free-route glm-5.1 calls (operator-authorized).
8	"""
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	import json, os, sys, time, re
11	_R = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
12	sys.path.insert(0, _R)
13	for line in open(os.path.join(_R, ".env"), encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore"):
14	    line = line.strip()
15	    if line and not line.startswith("#") and "=" in line:
16	        k, _, v = line.partition("="); os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip())
17	os.environ["PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT"] = "off"   # basket build free
18	os.environ["PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS"] = "1"           # free-route glm-5.1 (run condition)
19	os.environ["PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY"] = "16"               # FIX-C under test
20	os.environ["PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S"] = "45"            # the proven per-call deadline
21	os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.1")
22	os.environ.setdefault("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro")
23	MIN_CITED = int(os.getenv("GATE_MIN_CITED", "200"))
24	MAX_WALL_MIN = float(os.getenv("GATE_MAX_WALL_MIN", "30"))
25	
26	snap = json.load(open("outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/drb_78_parkinsons_dbs/corpus_snapshot.json", encoding="utf-8"))
27	pool = {}
28	for r in (snap.get("evidence_for_gen") or []):
29	    e = str((r or {}).get("evidence_id") or "").strip()
30	    if e: pool.setdefault(e, r)
31	from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data
32	gov = tuple(load_authority_data().get("psl_gov_suffixes") or ())
33	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import run_credibility_analysis
34	an = run_credibility_analysis(snap.get("question") or "", list(pool.values()), gov_suffixes=gov, domain=snap.get("domain") or None, judge=None)
35	from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import diagnose_unbound_supports_selection, build_verified_span_draft
36	wfe = diagnose_unbound_supports_selection(evidence_pool=pool, credibility_analysis=an, contract_plans=[])
37	raw = build_verified_span_draft(wfe.ev_ids, pool)
38	from src.polaris_graph.generator.live_deepseek_generator import _rewrite_draft_with_spans
39	rewritten, _c, _u = _rewrite_draft_with_spans(raw, pool)
40	print(f"[GATE] candidates={len(wfe.ev_ids)} draft_chars={len(raw)} | PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=16, REAL judge, ENFORCE", flush=True)
41	
42	os.environ["PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT"] = "enforce"
43	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import strict_verify
44	t0 = time.time()
45	rep = strict_verify(rewritten, pool)
46	wall = time.time() - t0
47	
48	kept = rep.total_kept
49	distinct = len({t.evidence_id for sv in rep.kept_sentences for t in (getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or [])})
50	
51	# junk heuristic on KEPT units (rough magnitude; manual sample below for truth)
52	_URL = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.|doi\.org|\.gov/|\.com/", re.I)
53	_SUBMIT = re.compile(r"\b(Received|Revised|Accepted|Published online|Correspondence|Reprints?)\b\s*[:.]", re.I)
54	_REFY = re.compile(r"\bet al\b|\bbibr\d|\b(19|20)\d\d[a-z]?\)|\bVol\.|\bpp?\.\s*\d")
55	def _is_junk(s: str) -> bool:
56	    return bool(_URL.search(s) or _SUBMIT.search(s) or len(_REFY.findall(s)) >= 2)
57	kept_texts = [getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "" for sv in rep.kept_sentences]
58	junk = [t for t in kept_texts if _is_junk(t)]
59	
60	print(f"\n[GATE] verify wall={wall:.1f}s ({wall/60:.1f} min) | kept_sentences={kept} distinct_cited={distinct} dropped={rep.total_dropped}", flush=True)
61	print(f"[GATE] suspected junk among kept (URL/submission-metadata/reference-list): {len(junk)} ({100*len(junk)//max(1,kept)}%)", flush=True)
62	print("\n--- 10 kept-unit samples (eyeball clinical vs junk) ---")
63	for t in kept_texts[:10]:
64	    print(f"   [{'JUNK?' if _is_junk(t) else 'clin '}] {t[:130]!r}")
65	print("\n--- 6 suspected-junk samples ---")
66	for t in junk[:6]:
67	    print(f"   {t[:140]!r}")
68	
69	problems = []
70	if wall/60 > MAX_WALL_MIN: problems.append(f"verify wall {wall/60:.1f}min > {MAX_WALL_MIN}min — FIX-C did not bound it")
71	if kept < MIN_CITED: problems.append(f"kept {kept} < {MIN_CITED} — enforce-path breadth below target")
72	print("\n" + "="*76)
73	if problems:
74	    for p in problems: print(f"   FAIL: {p}")
75	    print("[GATE][NO-GO]"); sys.exit(1)
76	print(f"[GATE][GO] enrichment verify COMPLETES in {wall/60:.1f}min (was ~173min serial) and KEEPS")
77	print(f"   {kept} cited sentences / {distinct} distinct sources on the REAL enforce path (target {MIN_CITED}).")
78	print(f"   junk leakage ~{100*len(junk)//max(1,kept)}% (separate quality follow-up; not a hang blocker).")
79	print("="*76); sys.exit(0)
80

content.text: Now let me apply the P0 fix. First the setdefault in run_s5_i3.py:

input.new_string:
for _k, _v in (
        ("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", "1"),
        ("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S", "300"),
    ):
        os.environ.setdefault(_k, _v)

    # P0 (2026-07-11 compose gear-loop iter 5): strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop ran SERIAL
    # in production because _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset
    # (provenance_generator.py:3390 read, serial gate at :3665), and NO compose launcher set the knob —
    # so the bounded, cost-reconciled, contextvars-copied ThreadPoolExecutor path (:3676-3723, already
    # gated at 16 by scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py) was DEAD in production while strict_verify
    # runs twice per section. Turn the parallel judge path ON with a bounded 8 workers. This is
    # faithfulness-NEUTRAL: the parallel path reassembles verdicts in ORIGINAL order, so kept/dropped is
    # byte-identical to the serial loop; only concurrency of in-flight judge calls changes. setdefault =>
    # an explicit launcher/env value still wins.
    os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY", "8")

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Compose gear-loop iter 5 — builds on iter 4 (section-level crash RESUME + self-relaunch). Two changes,
3	# both faithfulness-NEUTRAL (numeric forward-match + context-level NLI entailment UNCHANGED; the lexical
4	# >=2-word overlap gate stays DELETED at this branch head; nothing question-tuned):
5	#   (A) GEAR RULE (operator 2026-07-10): iter-4 HARDCODED the pinned cp3/cp4 paths. iter-5 RESOLVES the
6	#       NEWEST outline cp4 AND newest corpus cp3 at launch (ls -t) so the compose gear auto-picks up new
7	#       S3/S4 rounds as they land — the gears turn into each other. The pinned s4_gear/s3_gear ARE the
8	#       newest today, so pin + newest coincide; the dynamic resolve keeps that true on the NEXT S3/S4 round
9	#       without a launcher edit. Both resolved paths are PRINTED for the round report.
10	#   (B) 429 SELF-CONTENTION (observed in the iter-4 run 08:33-09:02: repeated OpenRouter 429 backoff +
11	#       a 3-min stall while a second heavy OpenRouter job ran in a sibling worktree). Drop the compose's
12	#       OpenRouter concurrency (PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 30->16, writer 24->12) so the gear loop coexists with
13	#       sibling jobs on the shared OpenRouter rate limit with FEWER backoffs -> sections FINALIZE and reach
14	#       the iter-4 per-section checkpoint sooner (a kill then loses less work). max_tokens slate unchanged
15	#       (a CAP, not a target -> reasoning is never starved; §9.1.8).
16	set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a
17	cd /workspace/compose_wt || exit 9
18	export PYTHONPATH=/workspace/compose_wt PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
19	# -- activation flags (ghost-free slate, iter-4 identical) --
20	export PG_COMPOSE_NO_RAW_SPAN_FALLBACK=1
21	export PG_SECTION_BASKET_MAP=1
22	export PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY=1
23	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER=1
24	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE=1
25	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED=1
26	export PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce
27	export PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE=1
28	export PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD=1
29	export PG_RENDER_SEAM_SANITIZE=1
30	# -- B4 resilience levers + throughput (iter-4 proven) --
31	export PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED=1
32	export PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS=1
33	# iter-5 (B): lower concurrency to cut 429 self-contention with sibling OpenRouter jobs.
34	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12
35	export PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16
36	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S=300
37	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S=1400
38	export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300
39	# iter-5 (Fable P0): the NLI verify pre-pass speedup (0615bc5) is capped at 4 by the process-global
40	# side-judge semaphore (judge_concurrency.py DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4, tuned for a credibility-burst
41	# 429 storm, not compose). 0 429s at 128-way proves rate limit is not the ceiling; raise the cap so
42	# verify threads don't queue behind 4 slots. Transport-only, faithfulness-NEUTRAL.
43	export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16
44	# iter-5 (Fable P1): a hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S. The
45	# code comment (entailment_judge.py:196) prescribes the run slate set this to 1 (=> up to 2 attempts on
46	# a hang vs the default 2 => 3x total_s of dead slot-hold). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (same fail-closed sentinel).
47	export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1
48	# -- §9.1.8 always-MAX token slate (z-ai/glm-5.2 real caps) --
49	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS=131072
50	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=65536
51	export PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000
52	export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2
53	# -- iter-5 (A): PINNED best inputs are the DEFAULT; the GEAR RULE resolves the NEWEST cp4+cp3 and uses
54	#    them when they are newer than the pin. Today pin == newest, so both resolve to s4_gear/s3_gear. --
55	CP2=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s2_hamster_i1/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json
56	PIN_CP3=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json
57	PIN_CP4=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_gear/cp4_outline_snapshot.json
58	NEW_CP4=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_*/cp4_outline_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
59	NEW_CP3=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_*/cp3_basket_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
60	CP4=${NEW_CP4:-$PIN_CP4}
61	CP3=${NEW_CP3:-$PIN_CP3}
62	OUT=/workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter5
63	CKPT=$OUT/ckpt
64	LOG=$OUT/compose.log
65	mkdir -p "$CKPT"
66	# -- FAIL-LOUD ghost-free pre-flight (operator P0 wiring lock) --
67	GHOST=$(grep -c writer_numeric_dropped src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py || true)
68	if [ "$GHOST" != "0" ]; then
69	  echo "[ABORT] ghost present: writer_numeric_dropped count=$GHOST" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 3
70	fi
71	if ! python -u -c "from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _synth_primary_enabled; import sys; sys.exit(0 if _synth_primary_enabled() else 7)"; then
72	  echo "[ABORT] _synth_primary_enabled() is False under this env slate" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 4
73	fi
74	echo "[preflight] ghost_absent(writer_numeric_dropped=0) AND synth_primary_enabled=True -> OK" | tee -a "$LOG"
75	{
76	  echo "=== iter-5 launch $(date -u) ==="
77	  echo "CP2=$CP2"; echo "CP3(resolved)=$CP3"; echo "CP4(resolved)=$CP4"
78	  echo "pin_cp3=$PIN_CP3 newest_cp3=$NEW_CP3"; echo "pin_cp4=$PIN_CP4 newest_cp4=$NEW_CP4"
79	  echo "commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)"
80	  echo "PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=$PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=$PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"
81	} | tee -a "$LOG"
82	for attempt in $(seq 1 8); do
83	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 present -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
84	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt START $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
85	  python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py     --cp2 "$CP2" --cp3 "$CP3" --cp4 "$CP4"     --out "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json"     --ckpt-dir "$CKPT"     --cap-primary 0 < /dev/null >> "$LOG" 2>&1
86	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt EXIT rc=$? $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
87	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 written -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
88	  sleep 5
89	done
90	echo "=== iter-5 loop finished $(date -u) cp5_exists=$([ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && echo yes || echo no) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
91

content.text: This worktree has concurrent uncommitted edits (launcher lines 39-47, multi_section_generator.py) that I did not make — a sibling change addressing the event-loop blocking via `asyncio.to_thread` (commit 0615bc5) plus side-judge concurrency. My P0 (PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY, which drives the ThreadPoolExecutor *inside* strict_verify's per-sentence loop) is orthogonal and complementary. I will add it without clobbering those edits.

input.new_string:
export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16
# P0 (2026-07-11, Fable): strict_verify's PER-SENTENCE entailment loop ran SERIAL in prod because
# _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset (provenance_generator.py:3390),
# and NO compose launcher set the knob -> the bounded, cost-reconciled ThreadPoolExecutor path
# (provenance_generator.py:3676-3723, already gated at 16 by scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py)
# was DEAD in production while strict_verify runs twice per section. Turn it ON at 8 in-flight judge
# calls. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: verdicts are reassembled in ORIGINAL order == byte-identical to serial.
# (Distinct from the off-loop asyncio.to_thread change and PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY above: this
# knob controls the intra-strict_verify per-sentence pool; the side-judge cap keeps those 8 from
# queueing behind the old 4-slot semaphore.)
export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8
